core.description: Always-installed foundation powering up AI use — guided git commits, web research, session close, and the always-on behavioral rules.

skills.description: Guided git commit with repo detection and message conventions
Multi-source web research with extraction and synthesis

Move/rename a file or folder in one git-aware operation that finds and fixes every reference — markdown links, wikilinks, frontmatter, config paths, and structural code imports (ast-grep, never regex). Stateless two-call consult→act with a drift-safe hash handshake; thin loader → safe-move-guide.md. The engine is a runnable Python package read in place from core/tools/safe-move (NOT installed into .claude/; see this module's tools inventory).

rules.description: Self-contained files with no content repetition
Transcription correction, name glossary, and batched confirmation
rules.stale_reason: Niche transcription-glossary loader; superseded by per-skill glossary loading in the meeting/therapy summarizers.

rules.description: Single-command Bash calls with no shell operators
rules.stale_reason: Obsolete under Claude auto-mode — the single-command / no-shell-operator constraint is no longer needed.

rules.description: Conclusion-first output, chunked presentation, no restating
Turn corrections into structural fixes that prevent recurrence
Detect and capture session context worth preserving
rules.stale_reason: Did not reliably trigger in practice; superseded by the session-close and compounding flows.

rules.description: Prefer available deterministic capabilities over LLM improvisation; compute (never estimate) exact-answer tasks; propose promoting recurring scripts into their workflow. Hard gate on the exact-answer arm, advisory elsewhere. Always active once installed.
Resolve output file paths via CLAUDE.md routing tables
Self-verification, critical partner mindset, root cause thinking
Always invoke matching skills before acting
Pre-dispatch gate for Agent tool calls — name matching skills explicitly in sub-agent prompts

tools.description: Runnable tool packages shipped in the RBTV source and run in place from {rbtv_path} — NOT installed into .claude/. The installer ignores this inventory key (admin/install/installer/manifest.py reads only skills/commands/rules/subagents per module), exactly like orchestration's model_packages/hooks; agents discover and invoke a tool via its owning skill loader. This block is a component-inventory record kept per the 'keep docs in sync' hard rule.

entries.name: safe-move
entries.runtime_prereq: ast-grep located via npx (powers code-reference matching; degrades gracefully with a code-backend-unavailable warning when absent — non-code references still work)
entries.description: Stateless, git-aware move/rename assistant with structural reference discovery. Two calls: consult (find + classify + risk-grade every reference, change nothing) then act (perform the git-aware move and auto-apply the safe fixes, surface the risky ones). Drift-safe via a per-reference hash handshake. Code references matched structurally via ast-grep, never regex.

office.description: Daily knowledge work — document export, legal advisory, meeting prep and summarization, client emails, and the problem-reframing + idea-sparring persona.

skills.description: Export markdown or HTML documents to styled PDF or DOCX
Guided meeting preparation — produces a strategic cheat sheet through classification, discovery, and generation
Process meeting transcripts into structured summaries with type-specific prompts and glossary integration
Voice principles for emails to clients, prospects, and partners — compaction-first, co-constructive stance, plain-text-friendly, with conditional pt-BR tonal realization

commands.description: Legal advisory persona for contract and compliance review

studio.description: HTML power-up — the studio loop entry via the strategist command (message-lock → design), visual design (HTML decks, image prompts, brand identity), design-token extraction from live sites, and browser automation.

skills.description: Browser automation via Playwright for testing and scraping
UI/UX design workflow with iterative screenshot refinement
Resolve hypresent review comments without deleting them — implement, reply inline, never resolve the human's thread

commands.description: Enter the studio loop — opens the Strategist (audience mode: investor, client, site-marketing, or app-product) who locks the message and hands off to Vivian for design
Extract design tokens and patterns from reference materials
Forensically analyze a static reference image into a strict JSON spec and ready-to-paste regeneration prompts for Nano-Pro, Flux, and Midjourney

orchestration.description: Long-horizon work orchestration — structured planning, plan execution via tiered sub-agents, and long-source mining.

skills.description: General orchestration — route tasks to the right worker, dispatch with self-contained artifacts + state spine, verify every return against disk, recover from halts; single front door including standalone CLI-model dispatch (kimi-code-cli/codex-cli/claude-code-cli/opencode). Thin loader → always-loaded core-protocol (invariants + situation table + installed-model capability summary) → JIT cards (intake, routing, dispatch-wrapper, halt-recovery, verification, state)
Structured planning workflow with iterative clarification

commands.description: Mine a long source via sub-agents to reconcile a target doc with user fixups (reconcile mode) or produce study notes (study mode) — orchestrator never reads the source directly

rules.description: Discoverability for rbtv-orchestrating — intake trigger bar (explicit ask, orchestration-flagged plan, ≥3 coordinated dispatches, multi-hour AFK, ≥2 worker types) + mid-task escalation (context filling, 3rd same-goal dispatch, cross-repo) + counter-list (single dispatch, quick lookups, <30-min, specialized-workflow-owned) that pulls orchestration-shaped work into the skill without the user naming it

model_packages.description: Per-model dispatch DOC packages (D18) for rbtv-orchestrating — NOT installed into .claude/; read just-in-time from orchestration/models/<model>/ in the RBTV source. install.py elects packages per workspace (--model-packages or the interactive picker) and records the selection in rbtv.json model_packages — the single election authority route.py reads; the orchestrating skill recalls it on demand via `python {rbtv_path}/orchestration/models/route.py --availability`. Nothing is baked into the shared repo. A directory is a model package iff it carries manifest.yaml (_fixture and mirror are infra, not packages). The shared API-worker engine at orchestration/models/_api/ (run.py runner + clients/) is infra — read JIT like _fixture/mirror, no manifest.yaml, not a model package; the API chat packages (deepseek, gemini) and the Agent-tool package (claude) run via it.

packages.name: kimi-code-cli
packages.description: Kimi CLI bounded-code executor (validated) — writes/runs code, commits locally; resume via session id

packages.name: codex-cli
packages.description: OpenAI Codex CLI worker (codex exec, probe-pending) — separate-process code execution

packages.name: claude-code-cli
packages.description: Claude headless worker (claude -p, validated) — native cwd-CLAUDE.md load; CLI-driven sub-conductor (process boundary clears the nesting wall)

packages.name: deepseek-api
packages.description: DeepSeek API chat / text-synthesis worker (probe-pending) — cheapest text, OpenAI-compatible JSON mode; runs via the shared models/_api/run.py runner, keys resolved from rbtv.json env_file

packages.name: gemini-api
packages.description: Gemini API chat worker (probe-pending) — light web grounding; runs via the shared models/_api/run.py runner

packages.name: claude-code-native
packages.description: Agent-tool Claude tiers opus/sonnet modeled as a routable package (validated) — sibling to claude-code-cli; in-session carrier, no native CLAUDE.md load, cannot spawn sub-agents (nesting wall)

packages.name: manus-api
packages.description: Manus agentic-web worker (probe-pending) — autonomous server-side browser/tool loop; per-task cost; raw-dump return only (no JSON envelope, no code cold-verify); runs via the shared models/_api/run.py runner

packages.name: opencode
packages.description: OpenCode CLI multi-provider worker (opencode run, probe-pending) — OSS harness serving the net-new z1 (zai/glm-5.2) + sakana (fugu-ultra) backends; NO native sandbox — worktree-mandatory confinement

hooks.description: PostToolUse hook(s) the orchestration install wires into the workspace's .claude/settings.local.json when the orchestration module is elected — read in-place from the RBTV source, never copied into .claude/. The wiring is performed in code by admin/install/installer/orchestration.py (sync_hook_entry on election, remove_hook_entry on de-election/cleanup), which identifies the rbtv-managed entry by sentinel (__rbtv__ == 'rbtv:context-monitor') OR command-path-signature (a command containing 'orchestration/hooks/context-monitor.py'), never a key alone — so the entry survives a Claude Code settings round-trip; hand-added hooks are preserved. This block is a component-inventory record; the wiring constants live in orchestration.py, not here.

entries.name: context-monitor
entries.description: Reads real context-window usage from the session transcript and emits a tiered refresh advisory during an orchestration run (self-detected from the transcript); silent in plain sessions and below 30%. Window configured via RBTV_CONTEXT_WINDOW (default 1000000). Feeds the real percentage into the state card's phase-boundary refresh checkpoint.

builder.description: Building RBTV itself — guided component creation/editing and the source-of-truth rule protecting installed copies.

skills.description: Scaffold new RBTV components (skills, commands, rules, workflows)

rules.description: RBTV repo is canonical — never edit installed copies

innovation.description: Business innovation frameworks, mentorship, and product discovery.

commands.description: Business innovation mentor with guided discovery frameworks
Product discovery persona for market fit and validation
Long-form writing persona with style adaptation
Analyze and extract tone profiles from text samples

coding.description: The evidence-gated, weight-graded done protocol for coding tasks. (Plain-language code communication moved to the new communication module; guided git commits moved to core; coding-discipline principles were generalized into the always-on reasoning rule.)

skills.description: On-fire body of the always-on rbtv-done-gate rule — thin loader that reads coding/skills/done-gate/done-gate-protocol.md (read JIT from {rbtv_path}, not installed): Contract/Exercise/Exhibit mechanics, Drivability Check + seam patterns, Fidelity Floor, Evidence Tier (Light inline / Full disk sheet), evidence-sheet format, Surprising Holds, Orchestrated Dispatches, Integrity Tripwire, anti-patterns. Invoked by the rule's Load Gate when a non-exempt coding task fires.

rules.description: Evidence-gated, weight-graded done for coding tasks — THIN always-on trigger layer (≈420 words): activation moments (Contract at task intake, Exercise+Exhibit at done-claim), the exempt list + fail-safe, the drivability-only exemption, the done-lock, and a MANDATORY Load Gate that invokes the rbtv-done-gate skill (the on-fire protocol body) the moment a non-exempt coding task starts. Evidence is weight-graded — a disk sheet for Full-tier work, the same rows inline in the done message for Light-tier. Exemption decisions stay in this always-on rule so 'this task is exempt' is never decided with the protocol unloaded. Always active once installed.

communication.description: Audience-adapted communication — general plain-language explanation for non-experts (define terms, no jargon, no analogies, no bare name-drops) plus the non-technical-user code-communication overlay. Elective and independent of the coding module.

rules.description: General plain-language layer — define every term, no analogies, no unexpanded acronyms, never a bare name-drop, state what a plan phase IS not just its label. Chat prose only. Always active once installed.

Code-communication overlay for non-technical users — pair every code identifier with a translation, frame coding decisions as behavior changes, ban raw output dumps; defers general plain-language to the plain-language rule and lead-with-decision to chat-discipline. Always active once installed.

caveman.description: Ultra-compressed caveman communication mode. Optional — token savings and fun. Based on JuliusBrussee/caveman.

rules.description: Caveman-speak linguistic transform (fragments, abbreviations, arrows, 1-3 lines) + auto-clarity escape — layered on chat-discipline (MECE: the behavioral filler/restating bans live in chat-discipline, not here)
Fun-mode parody commit messages in caveman voice — made for laughs, not serious repos
rules.stale_reason: Owner-marked opt-out 2026-06-13: parody commit voice is rarely wanted; keep the source but skip default install. Un-stale to re-enable.
